text: N daŋ tɛi nannakpila werge, baŋhina daŋ ɛɛ koorizenaa. I daŋ dɔɔ nannakpila werge, guŋ gil bá birgi hɛrkpila hina.
translated: The man went to the farm, and he saw the woman. The woman went to the farm, and she saw the man who was working.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.
translated: Then Zilpah bore Jacob a son.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb n wa na baa u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ni u mɛlikazi chɔɔllɛ keŋ!” U faa dau de sɔŋ Mahanam.
translated: Jacob said to his father, "Father, I have found a wild animal in the bush!" He took him to see it.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Awia, u ta newea, ba liu. U ta baabuu ba du u himmaa duula, di ɛɛ baal ni haaŋ.
translated: Awia, you are strong, but you are tired. You are carrying a heavy load, it is not easy for you.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Awia, nyiŋŋi daananaa di kuari muugberzenzeŋ bɛl, í wer di ra zuu u biini lugɔ dia. Nyiŋŋi kotaal ham u biini ni u harbɔɔ maa nɛnnɛ.
translated: The woman, she is going to the market to buy some food for her children. She is going to buy cornmeal, beans, and some meat.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: N daŋ tɛi nannakpila werge, baŋhina daŋ ɛɛ koorizenaa. I daŋ dɔɔ nannakpila werge, guŋ gil bá birgi hɛrkpila hina.
translated: I went to the market yesterday, and I bought some rice. Today I went to the market again, but I didn't buy anything.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.
translated: Then Zilpah bore a son to Jacob.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb n wa na baa u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ni u mɛlikazi chɔɔllɛ keŋ!” U faa dau de sɔŋ Mahanam.
translated: Jacob said to him, "You are a very foolish person!" He went and told Mahanam.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Awia, u ta newea, ba liu. U ta baabuu ba du u himmaa duula, di ɛɛ baal ni haaŋ.
translated: The woman, she is strong, but she is tired. She has worked hard all day, and now she is resting.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Awia, nyiŋŋi daananaa di kuari muugberzenzeŋ bɛl, í wer di ra zuu u biini lugɔ dia. Nyiŋŋi kotaal ham u biini ni u harbɔɔ maa nɛnnɛ.
translated: The woman, she is going to the market to buy some things, she will come back home in the evening. She will cook food for her children when she returns.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: N daŋ tɛi nannakpila werge, baŋhina daŋ ɛɛ koorizenaa. I daŋ dɔɔ nannakpila werge, guŋ gil bá birgi hɛrkpila hina.
translated: The old man went to the market, and he bought some kola nuts. When he came back from the market, he gave some kola nuts to his children.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.
translated: Zilpa was the maid of Leah, and she bore Jacob.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb n wa na baa u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ni u mɛlikazi chɔɔllɛ keŋ!” U faa dau de sɔŋ Mahanam.
translated: Jacob said to his father, "The wild animals are destroying our crops!" He went quickly to tell Mahanam.
